tokens: Okay, Joe, what's next on the agenda?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I copied your question, Dave, and stand by and take a breather.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave, if you'd just pick a site, I could sure start digging.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, why don't you do that?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; (I'll) just go after it (meaning the wrench) here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll be over at the Rover, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, we've got some instructions when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Rog, get Jim started on the ditching experiment, if you would please; and then I've got another good one to lay on you here.
Don't quite know how to explain it.
We'd like for you to try to get the deep core for us with the drill.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Jim laughs) Aw, Joe, you didn't even have to tell me, because I knew darn well that was coming with the stuff that we must be on top of here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Shall we take Station 8 with the (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...Jim is going to be digging at the same time.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Looking at his cuff checklist) Well, the thing is, do we want to do the whole Station 8 activity.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I guess if they want to do Station 8, they want to do Station 8.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, LRV is not parked in the right spot.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, let's just turn it around.
Lift it up an turn it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, they won't like that because the TV is all messed up then.
Let's just play it out here in front, right out over there (pointing west) and pretend like that is the side of the LRV, except the 16-millimeter won't be in the right spot.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pointing) We could aim the 16(-mm camera) out this way; out to the west.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, let me...Hey, Houston; what would you rather have?
16 millimeter movies of Station 8 or TV movies of Station 8?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Getting a little frustrated) You know, by the time we stand by, and you get to a decision (lost under Joe)...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Roger; and we'll take TV; that's plenty good enough.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We could turn the Rover around and...(Stops to listen to Joe)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...And, Dave, once you get him started, we can begin with the drill.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, I guess we want to really start the trench first, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, let me get the gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, and I'll get the comprehensive later, if we have a chance.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I better have your camera, because...Let's go out here where it's fresh.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me take the camera off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I'll take it, and I'll come do...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Joking) I know, you want me to dig down to that bedrock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You want me to dig down to bedrock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You remember how the Rover would normally be.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I'll get it (meaning the CDR's camera, which Jim has been using).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, a couple of comments here, and Jim as well.
We're going to be departing this site for the closeout in about 30 minutes.
So you're looking real good on the time, and we'll just pick up whatever we can.
No rush on any of it.
Dave, you'll want to think a minute about where that (drill) treadle probably is.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, I think I know where it is, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Thanks for getting me off to such a good start here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And take a little right turn there and let me get the down-Sun pre-picture here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Almost singing) While I go find my favorite little piece of gear.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You've got your favorite task and I've got mine.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Get it out of the way real quick.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, do you only want it 12 inches deep?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: What ever you think's reasonable, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The wall that I'm...Too bad the TV's there, Joe.
You can't see the (vertical, sunlit) wall.
Too bad, (because) the wall is very smooth.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The wall is fine, yet very cohesive.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we'll stand by for a verbal description, in fact.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I do find some small fragments: white fragments, black fragments.
I just exposed a very small fragment about 3 millimeters of a black glass.
But the wall that I've got here is only...No signs of layering at all.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Tell me when you're ready for pictures, Jimmy.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think I'm just about ready, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, let me take your pictures then.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Come over and see what you think.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh no, I think that you're not getting the penetrometer all the way down there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But not wide enough, you don't think?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I don't think it'll be big enough for the ears.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I hate to tell you that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Do you want to make a bet on that one?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: When I get down under the 12-inch layer, the surface is much harder, harder to dig through.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I also find more of the black glass fragments.
Much more cohesive down about...(Pause) Well, we ought to get a good sample at the bottom of this.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, it's easy to make a flat bottom because it's so hard.
I can see why Dave had a hard time drilling through it, now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing) You know, Jim, I got a checklist on the left arm for one thing that's going on now, and a checklist on the right arm for something else that's going on now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks like the Echo magazine worked okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's from me, and I'm going to put Foxtrot on the 16.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's stop with that one and document it.
We'll want samples from the bottom please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Gee, I think I've hit bedrock.
I think I've hit the bedrock!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I'm coming over right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, you might want to bring the SESC (Special Environmental Sample Container) from under the seat.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'll take a break while you photo, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Do you think it's long enough?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I really do think I'm almost down to bedrock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, we ought to have some good sample there from the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It looks like it has a little color change down there, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, maybe a slight...Seems to get a little darker...A little lighter, then a little darker.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: A shadow in the way here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Walls are just about vertical on the trench, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, we think you can collect the samples here pretty well.
And, Dave, in order to get that drill task accomplished, we're going to have to get you started on that shortly.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, he can't get the SESC very well by himself, I don't think, Joe.
It's tough for two of us to get.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: When you finish that, press on with the drill.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Jim) Okay, rotate it a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And while you're looking down in there, how deep do you think it is now?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Jim) Easy, don't lean the handle on me, I'll drop it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Answering Joe) Oh, I'd say it's 14 (to) 16 inches deep, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: A little bit more; keep coming.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Dave) Okay, you're going to leave me, and I'll sample it myself.
I guess I'll fill the bags myself then.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Brief pause) Unless you want to go do the drill.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll do all the...(Pause) Why don't you loan me your checklist.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: LM Crew: (Sustained, hearty laughter at the thought of Jim with three cuff checklists)
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: Yeah, I...(Stops to laugh) Oh, I think I can get...Joe'll talk me through it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Houston) Hey, which bag (SCB) do you want to put the SESC in, Joe, while I got it here?
(Pause) I'm sure you've been thinking of that.
(No answer) Well; (laughs) I didn't figure I'd get (a quick answer).
Here, I'm going to toss this one in there...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...for now so I can get going.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Joe, I'm going to do a little (laughing) sampling of the trench.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (If I can) just hang on to the (individual sample) bags.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Houston) Probably won't be quite as much of a sample here, since I'm doing it myself, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, is the SESC stowed now?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, it's in a seat pan right now; we'll get to it later, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's a perfect place; couldn't have suggested any better myself.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: R., as you probably know already, all we need from this EVA is really the hole in the ground.
The drill (battery) will probably give out on us during your rest period.
And, if need be, we can pull up whatever you get later on.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You wouldn't want to go on towards the North Complex without visiting the ALSEP site again, would you?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The soil samples from the bottom of the trench is in 252.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) And, Dave and Jim, we're coming up on fifteen minutes, "one-five" minutes, before closeout.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Roger, and you've done yeoman service.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I have to get in your way there, old buddy.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, that'd be a good idea.
I need to use this area.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, I'm going to skip sampling the side (of the trench), I'm just going to sample the top over here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Sounds good, if you don't see layering.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: So I can get on with the penetrometer.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Shadows really make a difference up here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Joe; the top of the trench, 253.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, Jim, you're in the way of the (16-mm) camera, old buddy.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah; if we're going to use all that (16-mm) film...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I got to get some bags here, man.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (a the treadle moves from under his right foot) Oh, shoot.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We can still see (with the TV).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Responding to Joe) Dave's talking about the other one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm going for the penetrometer.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Your (70-mm Hasselblad) camera's in your seat pan if you need it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, I don't think I'll...I can't take pictures and do penetrometer at the same time.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, you'll get a warning horn and you'll want to go over to your Aux Water shortly.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Grunting as he leans down) Okay, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, you'll want it to drop (meaning "drill" or "penetrate") into the ground as slowly as you can easily control.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Just in a hurry to get it done, and I just forgot your one inch per second; and I'll do that.
(Pause) (With a touch of self-criticism) Some days...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim, would you get my Aux Water?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I just can't feel in there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I know what you mean.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You want me to put you on Min Cooling at the same time?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I got the Min Cooling part, I just couldn't feel the Aux Water.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can get it; I'm right here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I got the Min Cooling.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Joe, I have the half-inch cone installed (on the penetrometer), and I'm going to...It's sitting on 1, I'm going to index it to 2.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, as soon as you get that (cable) unstuck, you'll want to back it off one and one-half turns.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) You can't bend over as far here as you can in one g.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, I hope you can watch this on TV.
The ground plate...I pull it down; and the spring is too strong.
It pulls the ground plate up.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...And I'm doing the one adjacent...I'm doing it adjacent to the trench...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: These (ground-plate) prints might stay here, Dave, so I can photograph them later.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (The imprints will show) where I took it.
Okay; that was adjacent to the trench.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm indexing to 3; and I'm going to do the trench bottom.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave, you might check your (16-mm) film mag, if you're back at the Rover now.
(Pause) And I'm talking about the DAC...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, it wouldn't have run out by now, Joe.
(Responding to Joe's last sentence) That's what I'm saying.
It wouldn't have run out by now.
I just turned it on; 12 frames per second, and it looks like it's 90 percent gone.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, hey, I need a call when my sublimator gets going, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; I'm in the trench bottom, and I'm pushing.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Joe) Okay, I'm starting for (drum index position) 4.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Dave) Okay, verify flags for me, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Going to higher cooling) Ohh, that feels so good!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'm going for the Rover tracks.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm on the very freshest Rover tracks...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And the one adjacent (to the Rover tracks)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...it's coming up on 5 minutes remaining before close out.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, adjacent to the Rover tracks.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) We don't want to leave here before I get a chance to collapse my trench, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Play it accordingly; and I thought that was my job.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Chuckles) (Pause) Okay, I'm going for the plate.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I see why we planned all this before we came.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Did I index it after the last one, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I don't think I indexed it after the last one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm indexing to six here for the trench bottom.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; here goes it for trench bottom.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; I'm going to be collapsing the trench side.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm about four inches out from the side of the trench.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'll take a quick picture here so you can see the location of all of those.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, are you working on the last stem there?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And I've got one last instruction for you here.
Using the drill, we want you to break it loose (meaning "free the drill string in the hole") and then let the drill and the stem sit there in the surface and we'll pull it out later.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And just leave the drill on stem, handle away from the Sun, as long as flutes pull free.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: As long as the threads pull free from the hole.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, Joe; we're in good shape.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We want the handle away from the Sun.
And we're ready for you to get back on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Grunting) I'm not sure I'll ever get it out.
(Pause) (The battery) box is sort of dirty.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, we want to end your tasks here, and we want you on the Rover, too, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let me take a few pictures here, and let me walk back.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Get pictures of the drill will you, Jim?
Take...Go...Hey, just south of the drill, I really need a (locator)...I already did a pan here.
Get your trench (pictures) and get a couple of pictures of the drill to show its position.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: A few pictures and you can walk back and, Dave, we want you to start on the Rover, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I think everything...You're not going to drive too fast are you?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'll meet you back there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Much dust when you drive fast.
transcript.speaker: Scott
